touches.ai_summary: Cold outreach to Derek Hill at MatrixVault regarding rep productivity challenges. Email highlighted how AI call analysis can help new CS leaders build coaching programs quickly. Derek responded expressing interest in learning more about automation capabilities.
next_steps: Schedule qualification call to understand current workflows

touches.ai_summary: 23-minute qualification call with Derek Hill. Discussed current state: team using Fireflies but lacking automation for follow-ups and CRM updates. Derek recently promoted to VP of CS, looking to scale coaching and reduce manual work for 6 reps. Confirmed budget authority sits with Javier Lima in Sales. Deal qualified with $6,200 ACV scope for unlimited automation tier.

questions_asked.question: How does your automation compare to what Fireflies offers?
questions_asked.answer: Fireflies focuses on transcription and basic summaries. We add automated CRM updates, follow-up email drafting, and deal risk scoring—eliminating 4-5 hours of manual work per rep weekly.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the need to upgrade from Fireflies now?
questions_asked.answer: I'm new to this role and need to move fast. Our reps spend too much time on admin work instead of customer conversations. I need automation that actually saves time, not just records calls.

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent with discovery questionnaire and demo prep materials. Derek responded within 24 hours with completed questionnaire, confirming 6 users, current Fireflies contract ending in 60 days, and priority on automation features. Expressed eagerness for demo.

questions_asked.question: Will we need to cancel Fireflies before starting, or can we run both during a trial?
questions_asked.answer: You can run both in parallel during a 14-day trial period. Most customers transition fully within the first week once they see the automation value.

next_steps: Include automation use cases for CS team

touches.ai_summary: 36-minute product demo with Derek Hill focusing on unlimited automation tier. Walked through automated CRM logging, follow-up email generation, and coaching insights dashboard. Derek engaged heavily on automation workflows and asked detailed questions about implementation timeline. Expressed concern about getting buy-in from Javier Lima (Sales Manager) who controls budget and has existing Fireflies relationship. No executive sponsor identified yet.

questions_asked.question: How long does implementation typically take for a team our size?
questions_asked.answer: For 6 seats, onboarding takes 3-5 days. We'll migrate your Fireflies data and configure automations during a white-glove setup session.

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in evaluating this—anyone from sales leadership or ops?
questions_asked.answer: Javier Lima in Sales manages our vendor budget. He'll need to approve, but I'm building the business case. I don't think we need to loop in the exec team for a tool purchase at this price point.

questions_asked.question: What's the pricing difference between your unlimited automation tier and Fireflies?
questions_asked.answer: Fireflies runs about $2,400/year for your team size. Our unlimited automation tier is $6,200 annually—higher upfront but saves 20+ hours per rep monthly, which pays back in productivity within 60 days.

objections: Concern about getting Javier's approval given existing Fireflies relationship and higher price point
next_steps: Derek to brief Javier and share demo recording
Schedule meeting with Javier to discuss budget and ROI

touches.ai_summary: 27-minute meeting with Derek Hill to discuss proposal. Derek confirmed Javier is supportive but wants to see a detailed ROI comparison against Fireflies before final approval. Conversation focused on negotiating contract terms and implementation timeline. Derek mentioned pressures from limited CS budget and needing to show quick wins in his new role. Still no executive sponsor engaged—Derek positioning this as a departmental tool purchase rather than strategic initiative.

questions_asked.question: Can you provide customer references specifically from CS teams that switched from Fireflies?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely. I'll connect you with two CS leaders who made the switch and saw 40%+ reduction in admin time within the first month.

questions_asked.question: What timeline are you working toward for going live?
questions_asked.answer: Ideally we'd start before end of quarter so I can show productivity gains in my Q2 review. Our Fireflies contract renews in 45 days, so that's the natural cutover point.

questions_asked.question: Is there any flexibility on the $6,200 price given we're already spending on Fireflies?
questions_asked.answer: I need to stay within our current tool budget if possible. What's the best you can do if we commit for annual instead of monthly?

objections: Price sensitivity—requesting discount to stay within existing Fireflies budget allocation
next_steps: Send ROI comparison doc and customer references
Javier to review proposal by end of week
Derek to confirm final decision timeline

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent with ROI calculator showing $18,400 annual productivity value vs $6,200 cost, customer references from similar CS teams, and revised proposal. Included note addressing pricing question—explained annual commit locks in current rate with unlimited automation tier. Derek acknowledged receipt and confirmed Javier is reviewing this week, expects to have decision by Monday.

questions_asked.question: If we move forward, can we add more seats mid-contract as we grow the team?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, you can add seats anytime at prorated pricing. Most customers expand within 6 months once they see the productivity impact.

next_steps: Derek and Javier to discuss internally
Prepare contract for signature pending approval
